content.problemStatement:
For a non-negative integer XXX, the function G(X)G(X)G(X) is defined as the greatest common divisor of all the integers generated by all submasks of the binary representation of XXX, i.e. integers obtained by replacing some (possibly none or all) 111-s in this binary representation by 000-s.

You are given an integer NNN. Find S=∑X=1NXG(X)S = \sum_{X=1}^N X^{G(X)}S=∑X=1N​XG(X) modulo 998,244,353998,244,353998,244,353.
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first and only line of each test case contains an integer NNN.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print a single line containing one integer SSS modulo 998,244,353998,244,353998,244,353.
